data.title: Anthropic closes on $2.5B credit facility as Claude continues to impress investors
data.description:
Anthropic, an artificial intelligence public benefit corporation, continues to attract investors as it closed on a $2.5B, five-year revolving credit facility this week.

"This credit facility further strengthens our balance sheet and provides us with the flexibility to continue our exponential growth while maintaining our commitment to responsible AI development," said Anthropic Chief Financial Officer Krishna Rao, in a post Friday afternoon on LinkedIn. "The backing of these global financial institutions is a testament to the strength of our business and the resonance of our mission. We continue to be well-positioned to accelerate our research at the frontier of AI, expand our product capabilities, and deliver substantial value to our rapidly growing customer base."

Morgan Stanley, Barclays, Citibank, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Royal Bank of Canada and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group all participated in the credit facility.

This follows a $3.5B funding round the company wrapped up in early March, which propelled its valuation to $61.5B at the time.

Anthropic, which is backed by tech titans such as Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL), has found commercial success through enterprises utilizing its Claude family of large language models. Companies such as Zoom (ZM), Snowflake (SNOW) and Pfizer (PFE) use Claude to help transform their operations. It also powers Amazon's virtual assistant Alexa. 

Earlier this month, it was reported Apple (AAPL) is teaming up with Anthropic to develop an AI-powered platform which will assist developers in writing, editing and testing code.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: NVDA, MAIN,
data.description:
Here are the latest upgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

NVDA (Nvidia). Sell to hold by Cavenagh Research. "Despite earlier concerns, demand-related downside risks have largely proven unfounded. And with investor sentiment hitting a low in May, I expect a recovery in the second half of 2025, supported by strong underlying fundamentals."

Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN). Sell to hold by REITer's Digest. "After a sharp 20% decline and NAV growth, the Company now trades near its historical average valuation, making it more attractive again."

Albemarle (NYSE:ALB). Sell to hold by Sensor Unlimited. 

AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP). Hold to buy Richard Durant.

Hims & Hers (NYSE:HIMS). Hold to buy by Stephen Ayers.

Antero Resources (NYSE:AR). Hold to buy by Patient Tech Investor. 

Anavex Life Sciences (NASDAQ:AVXL). Hold to buy by Lane Simonian.

data.title: OpenAI may be part of new 5GW data center project in UAE: report
data.description:
• OpenAI, the generative artificial intelligence startup backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), could become a “key anchor” of the new 5 gigawatt data center project unveiled this week in the United Arab Emirates, Bloomberg reported.
  • Other U.S. firms and UAE-based MGX could also participate in the project, the news outlet added.
  • The new AI campus — the largest outside the U.S. — will be the home of several U.S. hyperscalers and large companies that can use the capacity for regional computing power, the Department of Commerce said in a statement issued on Thursday.
  • The campus will be built by UAE-based firm G42 and operated in partnership with numerous unnamed U.S. companies. G42 already has a relationship with various U.S. companies, including Nvidia (NVDA) and Microsoft.
  • The unveiling of the 5 gigawatt data center project occurred during President Trump's visit to the Middle East.

data.title: UnitedHealth rebounds amid insider purchases
data.description:
Shares of UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) bounced back on Friday, ending an eight-day losing streak marked by multiple setbacks as several of its directors disclosed the purchase of more than $1.5M worth of company stock.

According to regulatory filings submitted on Friday, UnitedHealth’s (NYSE:UNH) directors, Tim Flynn, Kristen Gil, and John Noseworthy, bought about 5.5K UNH shares across three transactions worth approximately $491.8K, $1.0M, and $93.6K, respectively, adding to their existing holdings.

Kristen Gil, a senior executive at Alphabet (GOOG), who conducted the biggest transaction of the three, has purchased 3.7K UNH shares for $271.70 apiece in a transaction worth over $1M, expanding her holding by over threefold.

The insider purchases took place on May 14 and May 15 as UNH stock kept falling to new 52-week lows over the past few days amid concerns over leadership changes and regulatory risks.

On Tuesday, the company withdrew its full-year outlook following the abrupt departure of its CEO, Andrew Witty, following which its shares sank to a 52-week low. A day later, allegations about a criminal probe initiated by the Department of Justice against UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) surfaced, sending the stock to a five-year low on Thursday. Despite today’s uptick, UNH remains ~40% down from its recent peak in November.

data.title: U.S. stocks rise as investors digest softer inflation data, sentiment report
data.description:
Wall Street major averages rose in Friday's early afternoon trade despite softer-than-expected inflation data, and consumer sentiment dropping to the second-lowest on record.

The University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index edged down to 50.8 in May, down from 52.2 in April. Consensus was 53.1. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last up 0.5%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was 0.6%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last 0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 1 basis point at 4.45%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was also up 1 basis point at 3.99%.  

Week-to-date, the U.S. averages are on track to take home gains. The S&P 500 is up 2.4% since the start of the week; the Dow is up 0.7%, and the Nasdaq is up 3.4%.

“Markets continued to advance over the last 24 hours, as weak U.S. inflation data and lower oil prices raised expectations that the Federal Reserve would still cut rates this year,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

A key factor driving the rally was the U.S. PPI data for April, which showed inflation pressures were much softer than expected.

“Thursday's softer-than-expected PPI inflation print helps to confirm that inflation is moving in the right direction, especially after Tuesday's soft CPI report,” said Clark Bellin, president and chief investment officer at Bellwether Wealth. “It's encouraging that the inflation data has softened in April, especially as we brace for the potential for higher inflation over the coming months as tariffs work their way through the economy.”

Healthcare companies were among Friday's top movers, including Moderna (MRNA), 6.8%; UnitedHealth, up 5.4%; and Humana (HUM), up 4.2%. Also, Archer Aviation is up 9.8% on the back of strong Q1 results, improved liquidity, and strategic partnerships.

More on the economic side, U.S. import and export prices surprised to the upside in April, housing starts rose in April, and building permits dropped more than expected.

data.title: U.S. rig count continues to edge lower in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of total drilling rigs in the U.S. fell for the third straight week, down by 2 to 576, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest weekly survey.

The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. shed 1 to 473 this week, their lowest level since January, gas rigs also dropped 1 to 100, and 3 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin fell by 3 to 279 last week, the oil rig count in the Eagle Ford gained 1 to 40, and oil rigs in the Williston Basin stayed flat at 32.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Bitcoin to rise W/W as market sentiment improves on U.S.-China trade talks, soft inflation data
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is expected to rise marginally, 1.1% on a weekly basis, as market sentiments seem to improve on the back of U.S.-China trade talks and softer inflation prints.

The world’s oldest digital currency traded above the $100K mark throughout the week and briefly went above the $105K threshold on Monday.

Wall Street on Monday also soared after the U.S.-China trade talks.

Over the weekend, the U.S. and China agreed to a significant reduction in tariffs on each other’s goods for 90 days. Each country will cut tariffs by 115%, with the U.S. cutting tariffs on Chinese imports from 145% to 30% and China cutting tariffs on U.S. imports from 125% to 10%.

Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) momentum continued as both the retail and wholesale inflation numbers came in softer than expected.

“We believe that institutional investors are less apprehensive about investing in Bitcoin and crypto as U.S.-China trade talks come to a conclusion and the likelihood of rate cuts increases,” Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE, said.

Right now, macro concerns are driving the market and the next Federal Reserve decision and outlook remarks in June will likely be the key factor in driving Bitcoin past its previous all-time high. This would stimulate lending and investment in the U.S.  economy and hopefully drive growth, avoiding the recession that investors are apprehensive about, Mei added.

On Wednesday, former J.P. Morgan strategist Marko Kolanovic had strong words for those touting the global race for crypto supremacy as he continued to slam the asset class. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) however did not seem to be impacted much by Kolanovic’s words.

Meanwhile, some analysts continued to stay positive on the cryptocurrency.

“Bitcoin's fundamentals, such as scarcity and adoption by institutions, continue to strengthen, making it a viable alternative to gold,” Seeking Alpha analyst Nicholas Thomas said.

“Key technical levels indicate Bitcoin is retesting crucial zones, with potential for higher highs if it stays above the yearly open. A pullback is normal given the recent rally, but I remain bullish unless Bitcoin breaks key levels or fundamentals shift negatively,” Thomas added.

More Notable News

The Securities and Exchange Commission has been investigating whether crypto exchange Coinbase Global (COIN) misstated its user numbers in past financial statements.

Coinbase Global (COIN) expects to pay $180M-$400M in reimbursements and remediation after a recent data breach exposed sensitive customer information and led to users being tricked into sending funds to the attackers.

Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) will be added to the constituents of the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) on May 19, S&P Dow Jones Indices said.

Hyperscale Data (GPUS) said through its unit, Sentinum, it plans to resume Bitcoin mining operations at one of its two Montana sites on or around June 10.

eToro—the Israel-based stock and cryptocurrency trading platform—priced its IPO at $52 per share, which is above its originally anticipated range of $46 to $50.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) agreed to acquire WonderFi Technologies (OTCQB:WONDF) (WNDR:CA) for a total equity value of C$250M (US$179M), boosting Robinhood's crypto business in Canada.

Gryphon Digital Mining (GRYP) announced an all-stock merger with American Bitcoin, a Bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining firm backed by U.S. President Donald Trump's two eldest sons.

Strategy (MSTR) said it acquired 13,390 Bitcoins (BTC-USD) in the week ended May 11, for a total of $1.34 billion.

Crypto Moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose 0.1% to $104.1K on Friday as of 2:15 pm ET. Ether (ETH-USD) up 0.3% to $2.6K. Doge (DOGE-USD) rose 0.4%.
